name: @radzor/uptime-monitor
description: Monitor URL uptime with configurable check intervals, latency tracking, and status change alerts. Supports multiple targets, pause/resume, and downtime history. Zero dependencies — uses raw fetch.

inputs.name: timeout
inputs.description: HTTP request timeout in milliseconds per check.

inputs.name: latencyThresholdMs
inputs.description: Latency threshold in ms; exceeding it fires onLatencySpike.

inputs.name: headers
inputs.description: Default HTTP headers sent with each check request.

outputs.name: uptimeReport
outputs.description: Status report for a monitored target.

fields.name: uptimePercent
fields.description: Uptime percentage based on check history.

events.name: onDown
events.description: Fired when a target goes down (first failure after being up).

events.name: onRecovered
events.description: Fired when a target recovers after being down.

events.name: onLatencySpike
events.description: Fired when response latency exceeds the configured threshold.

actions.name: addTarget

params.name: intervalMs
params.description: Override the default check interval for this target.

actions.description: Add a URL to the monitoring list and start checking it.

actions.name: removeTarget
actions.description: Remove a target and stop its checks.

actions.name: getStatus

params.name: url
params.description: URL to get status for. If omitted, returns all targets.

actions.description: Get the current status report for one or all targets.

actions.name: pause
actions.description: Pause monitoring for a target (or all if URL omitted).

actions.name: resume
actions.description: Resume monitoring for a paused target (or all if URL omitted).

connectsTo.description: Alert team when a monitored service goes down

llm.constraints: Uses raw fetch() for HTTP checks — the target must be reachable from the server. Check intervals are managed via setInterval with unref() to avoid keeping the process alive. A target is considered 'down' if fetch throws or the HTTP status is >= 500. Status codes 2xx-4xx are considered 'up'.
